title: Right Relationship.To play the media you will need to either update your browser to a recent version or update yourFlash plugin
justification: This talk directly addresses 'Right Relationship' as its explicit theme and provides the most comprehensive treatment, weaving together the ten perfections (paramis) as expressions of relationship in the world. It offers rich practical examples (the aunt caring for Uncle John, the surgeon's compassion, gift-giving dynamics), explores the psychology of attachment versus love, and maintains excellent narrative flow with Bhante's characteristic voice and humor. The structure moves logically from dana/generosity through morality, renunciation, wisdom, energy, patience, truthfulness, resolution, loving-kindness, and equanimity—providing a natural spine for grafting complementary material.
summary: This talk explores how the Buddha's teachings on the ten perfections (paramis) express themselves through our relationships, examining how generosity, compassion, patience, and equanimity transform our connections with others. Bhante uses vivid personal anecdotes and practical examples to illustrate how right relationship emerges from both insight practice and the cultivation of wholesome attitudes toward all beings.

relevance.note: Directly addresses right relationship through ten pāramīs
